# How to View Your Earnings

Track your Fleet Feast income, understand payment timelines, and manage your vendor finances effectively.

## Accessing Your Earnings Dashboard

1. Log in to your vendor account
2. Click "Vendor Dashboard"
3. Select "Earnings" from the menu
4. View real-time financial data

## Earnings Dashboard Overview

### At-a-Glance Summary

**Current Period (This Month):**
- Total bookings completed
- Gross revenue
- Platform fees
- Net earnings
- Pending payouts

**Year-to-Date:**
- Total completed bookings
- Annual revenue
- Total fees paid
- Net annual income
- Average booking value

**Lifetime Stats:**
- Total events served
- Total revenue generated
- Platform tier (Standard 15% or Loyalty 10%)
- Customer satisfaction rating

## Understanding Your Earnings

### Payment Timeline

```
Event Date → 7 Days Hold → Payout Released → Bank Deposit
                                              (1-3 business days)
```

**Example:**
- Event: June 15
- Payout released: June 22
- In your bank: June 23-25

### Booking Value Breakdown

For each booking, you'll see:

**Gross Booking Amount:** $500
- Subtotal: $500
- Customer pays: $500 + service fee

**Your Earnings:**
- Gross: $500
- Platform fee (15%): -$75
- **Net to you: $425**

**Payment Schedule:**
- Deposit (50%): $250 → on booking confirmation
- Final (50%): $250 → 72 hours before event
- **Total held: $500** → released 7 days after event
- **You receive: $425** → 8-10 days after event

### Platform Fee Tiers

**Standard (15%):**
- Default rate for all vendors
- Covers platform, payment processing, support
- Transparent and fair

**Loyalty (10%):**
- Achieved at 50 completed bookings
- 5% savings on every booking
- Lifetime benefit once achieved

**Example Savings:**
- $1000 booking at 15%: You keep $850
- $1000 booking at 10%: You keep $900
- **Savings: $50 per $1000 booking**

## Earnings Reports

### Transaction History

**View All Transactions:**
- Date of transaction
- Booking ID
- Customer name (anonymized)
- Gross amount
- Platform fee
- Net amount
- Payout status

**Filter Options:**
- Date range
- Payout status (pending, released, deposited)
- Booking type
- Amount range

**Sort Options:**
- Most recent first
- Highest value first
- By payout date
- By event date

### Pending Payouts

**Currently Held:**
Lists all bookings where:
- Event has occurred
- Payment is in 7-day hold
- Awaiting release

**Information Shown:**
- Event date
- Expected payout date
- Gross amount
- Net amount after fees
- Days until release

### Completed Payouts

**Payment History:**
- All payouts sent to your bank
- Date deposited
- Amount
- Included bookings
- Bank account (last 4 digits)

### Monthly Statements

**Auto-Generated:**
- Created on 1st of each month
- Previous month's activity
- Total earnings
- Total fees
- Net income
- PDF download available

**Use For:**
- Tax preparation
- Accounting records
- Financial planning
- Business analysis

### Tax Documents

**Annual 1099 (if applicable):**
- Available by January 31
- Shows total annual income
- Required if you earned $600+
- Downloadable PDF

**Important:**
- You're an independent contractor
- Responsible for your own taxes
- Report all income
- Consult tax professional

## Payment Methods

### Bank Account Setup

**Link Your Bank:**
1. Dashboard → Settings → Payment Info
2. Enter bank account details
3. Verify with micro-deposits
4. Set as primary payout method

**Required Information:**
- Bank name
- Routing number
- Account number
- Account type (checking/savings)

**Security:**
- Encrypted and secure
- Processed by Stripe
- PCI compliant
- Your data protected

### Changing Bank Accounts

**Update Payout Method:**
1. Add new bank account
2. Verify with micro-deposits
3. Set as primary
4. Future payouts go to new account

**Note:**
- Pending payouts go to originally scheduled account
- Allow 3-5 days for verification
- Can't change account for in-flight payments

### Backup Payment Method

**Add Secondary Account:**
- Acts as backup if primary fails
- Ensures you always get paid
- Easy to switch between accounts

## Tracking Performance

### Revenue Metrics

**Average Booking Value:**
- Total revenue ÷ number of bookings
- Helps price competitively
- Track trends over time

**Monthly Revenue Trends:**
- Chart showing income by month
- Identify peak seasons
- Plan for slow periods
- Set realistic goals

**Event Type Breakdown:**
- Which events generate most revenue?
- Corporate vs. private vs. wedding
- Focus on most profitable types

### Booking Analytics

**Acceptance Rate:**
- Bookings accepted ÷ total requests
- Target: 70%+ for best search ranking
- High rate = more visibility

**Conversion Rate:**
- Profile views → booking requests
- Higher = more effective profile
- Improve with better photos, pricing

**Repeat Customer Rate:**
- % of customers who book again
- Indicator of satisfaction
- More repeats = stable income

### Financial Goals

**Set Targets:**
- Monthly revenue goal
- Number of bookings goal
- Average booking value target
- Yearly income goal

**Track Progress:**
- Real-time progress bars
- Weekly/monthly comparisons
- Year-over-year growth
- Celebrate milestones!

## Maximizing Earnings

### Pricing Strategies

**Optimize Pricing:**
- Research competitor rates
- Adjust for quality/experience
- Test different price points
- Premium pricing for peak times

**Package Deals:**
- Bronze/Silver/Gold tiers
- Bundle services for higher value
- Create signature packages
- Upsell add-ons

**Dynamic Pricing:**
- Higher rates for weekends
- Peak season pricing (wedding season)
- Last-minute booking premiums
- Off-peak discounts

### Increase Booking Volume

**Improve Visibility:**
- Complete profile (ranks higher)
- High-quality photos
- Fast response times
- Excellent reviews

**Expand Availability:**
- More days available
- Wider service area
- Flexible minimums
- Accept more event types

**Promotions:**
- First-time customer discount
- Referral bonuses
- Seasonal specials
- Corporate packages

### Upselling Strategies

**Add-Ons:**
- Extra menu items
- Extended service time
- Additional staff
- Premium ingredients
- Beverage packages

**Before Event:**
- Suggest upgrades
- Offer dessert packages
- Premium bar options
- Custom decorations

**During Event:**
- Professional photos
- Extended hours
- Additional servings
- Merchandise sales

### Reduce Costs

**Platform Fees:**
- Reach loyalty tier (10% vs 15%)
- 50 bookings to qualify
- Lifetime benefit

**Operating Costs:**
- Efficient route planning
- Bulk ingredient purchases
- Reduce food waste
- Optimize staffing

**Time Management:**
- Book multiple events per day
- Reduce travel distance
- Streamline setup/breakdown
- Automate where possible

## Financial Planning

### Cash Flow Management

**Understand Timing:**
- Deposit received at booking (held)
- Final payment 3 days before event
- Payout 7 days after event
- Bank deposit 1-3 days later

**Total Time:**
- Booking → money in bank = 10-14 days post-event
- Plan cash flow accordingly

**During Hold Period:**
- Money exists but not accessible
- Plan for this delay
- Don't count it until deposited

### Budgeting

**Fixed Costs:**
- Insurance
- Permits/licenses
- Vehicle maintenance
- Equipment
- Marketing

**Variable Costs:**
- Ingredients (per event)
- Staff (per event)
- Travel/fuel
- Packaging/supplies

**Calculate Profit:**
```
Booking Revenue:        $500
Platform Fee (15%):     -$75
Food Costs (30%):       -$150
Labor (20%):            -$100
Travel/Other (10%):     -$50
------------------------
Net Profit:             $125 (25% margin)
```

### Tax Planning

**Quarterly Estimates:**
- Set aside 25-30% for taxes
- File quarterly estimated taxes
- Avoid year-end surprises
- Consult CPA

**Deductible Expenses:**
- Ingredients and supplies
- Vehicle expenses
- Insurance
- Permits and licenses
- Marketing
- Platform fees
- Equipment
- Fuel/travel

**Record Keeping:**
- Save all receipts
- Track mileage
- Document expenses
- Use accounting software
- Quarterly reviews

### Growth Planning

**Reinvestment:**
- New equipment
- Marketing campaigns
- Second truck
- Staff expansion
- Menu development

**Savings Goals:**
- Emergency fund (3-6 months expenses)
- Equipment replacement fund
- Expansion fund
- Retirement savings

## Troubleshooting

### Payment Not Received

**Check:**
1. Has it been 7 days since event?
2. Is bank account verified?
3. Review transaction status
4. Check spam folder for emails

**Still Missing:**
- Contact vendor support
- Provide booking ID
- Verify bank account details
- Allow 24-48 hours for investigation

### Incorrect Amount

**Verify:**
- Platform fee calculation
- Booking total
- Any adjustments or refunds
- Customer modifications

**Dispute:**
- Email vendors@fleetfeast.com
- Include booking ID
- Explain discrepancy
- Provide expected vs. actual amounts

### Bank Account Issues

**Verification Failed:**
- Re-check routing/account numbers
- Ensure account is active
- Contact your bank
- Try re-verification

**Payment Rejected:**
- Verify account is open
- Check account type matches
- Update bank information
- Contact support for manual processing

### Tax Document Questions

**1099 Issues:**
- Should receive by Jan 31
- Check earnings > $600
- Update mailing address
- Request reissue if needed
